alt: Let agents see. Keep network control. Crawl, search, and normalize evidence without exposing an unrestricted browser.

hook: Choose the security boundary before the first request.
alt: Two crawl tiers. One honest boundary. Use DNS-pinned local crawling for untrusted public URLs. Use the small serverless tier only for origins you own or trust.

hook: No cookie extraction. Authority stays visible.
alt: Know which sources are ready. Run one local doctor command before a workflow touches the web, an API, or an optional read session.

hook: A crawler should know when to stop.
alt: Every discovery path spends a budget. Creator-owned limits cover pages, depth, bytes, requests, concurrency, and deadline.

hook: Read-only by default. Evidence stays attributable.
alt: Give agents eyes— with boundaries. The local tier validates DNS answers, pins the admitted address, and re-checks every redirect hop.
See the web. Stay bounded. Put hard limits around every read and discovery path.

hook: For developers building evidence-aware agent workflows.
alt: Web evidence that names its boundary. Normalize public pages and read-only provider results into records a workflow can inspect and cite.

hook: No claim of universal safety. The boundary is documented.
alt: A crawler that fails closed. Private targets, unsafe redirects, expired budgets, and disallowed routes stop before extraction.
Building an agent crawler that fails closed DNS admission, redirect checks, robots, and creator-owned budgets.
Two crawl tiers. One explicit security model. A hardened local path for untrusted URLs and a restricted serverless path for owned or trusted origins.
From public URL to normalized record. Keep the route, retrieval metadata, extracted content, and source identity visible to the workflow.
Your crawler should know when to stop. Hard ceilings for pages, depth, bytes, requests, concurrency, and time.
Public-web reach. Checked first. Source doctor → bounded crawl → normalized evidence
Bound the crawl. Keep the evidence. A policy-aware crawl path plus public, official, no-key, and session-backed read routes.
